title: No Visas for Anti-Semitic Students Act
short_title: No Visas for Anti-Semitic Students Act
plain_summary: This bill gives the government power to cancel or refuse student visas for foreign students who do bad things to Jewish people. The bad things include hurting Jewish people, bothering them, damaging their property, or helping others do these things. The government says these actions could hurt America's relationships with other countries. If foreign students do these things, they cannot get or keep their student visas to study in America.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the No Visas for Anti-Semitic Students Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Visa Revocation or Denial for Students Engaging in Antisemitic Activities
sections.text: The Secretary of State must cancel or refuse to give student visas to foreign students who do certain anti-Jewish activities. This applies to F visas (regular students) and M visas (vocational students) under immigration law. The Secretary must take action if two things happen. First, the student must engage in prohibited anti-Jewish conduct as defined in this law. Second, the Secretary must decide that this conduct could cause serious problems for America's relationships with other countries. The law defines antisemitism using the same definition that the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance adopted on May twenty sixth two thousand sixteen. The United States is a member of this group and the State Department has adopted this definition. This includes the examples of modern antisemitism that the group identified. Prohibited anti-Jewish conduct means two types of actions. The first type is physically attacking, damaging property, or harassing a Jewish person, their belongings, community buildings, or religious buildings. The person doing this must intend to scare or hurt someone because they are Jewish or connected to Jewish people or places. The second type is giving help to someone else to do these attacks. This help can include money, planning, or encouraging violence. The person giving help must know that the other person plans to use this help to attack Jewish people or property.

sections.title: Section 3 Terms of Nonimmigrant Status for Students
sections.text: This section changes the Immigration and Nationality Act by adding new rules for student visas. A foreign person cannot get or keep a student visa (F or M type) if they do prohibited anti-Jewish conduct and the Secretary of State decides this conduct could cause serious problems for America's relationships with other countries. If the Secretary finds that someone who already has a student visa has done prohibited anti-Jewish conduct, and this conduct or person could cause serious foreign policy problems for America, then the Secretary must cancel their visa. The section uses the same definitions as Section 2. Antisemitism means the definition that the International Holocaust Remembrance Alliance adopted on May twenty sixth two thousand sixteen, which the State Department has also adopted. This includes the modern examples of antisemitism that the group identified. Prohibited anti-Jewish conduct has two parts. First, it means physically attacking, damaging property, or harassing a Jewish person, their belongings, community buildings, or religious buildings with the goal of scaring or hurting them because they are Jewish or connected to Jewish people or places. Second, it means giving help like money, planning, or encouragement to someone else, knowing that person will use this help to attack Jewish people or property.
